quotes.text: Your SaaS idea is probably a spreadsheet. Ship the spreadsheet first.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: Nothing is more devastating to a startup than building something nobody wants. And nothing is more common.
quotes.author: Unknown

quotes.text: The best validation is someone giving you money. Everything else — surveys, waitlists, likes — is just flirting.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's nightmare isn't failure. It's spending 2 years building something that gets 3 users.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate a SaaS idea is to sell it before you build it. If nobody buys, save yourself 6 months.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an indie project is to pre-sell it. If nobody pays, save yourself the build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Building a tool you'd pay for is easy. Getting others to pay for it is hard. The gap between these is where indie hackers live.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an idea is to charge for it before it exists. If nobody pays, you've saved months.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate your indie project is to pre-sell it. If nobody pays, save yourself the build.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The best way to validate an indie idea is to build a landing page, collect emails, and see who signs up.
quotes.author: based
